title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday marking the start of the new calendar year with celebrations across Peru.

festivals.name: Marinera Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of Peru's national dance the Marinera with competitions and performances along the northern coast.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Sebastián
festivals.description: Traditional festival honoring Saint Sebastian with colorful processions, dance, and local music in the Cusco highlands.

festivals.name: Carnaval Amazónico
festivals.description: Vibrant carnival celebration in the Amazon basin featuring water battles, music, and indigenous traditions.

festivals.name: Carnival of Puno
festivals.description: Colorful pre-Lenten carnival featuring traditional dances, elaborate costumes, and Andean music traditions.

festivals.name: Carnival of Cajamarca
festivals.description: Traditional highland carnival with water throwing, parades, and colorful local costumes celebrating regional identity.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Candelaria
festivals.description: One of South America's most spectacular religious festivals blending Catholic and indigenous traditions with elaborate processions and dance competitions.

festivals.name: Día de la Independencia
festivals.description: National holiday celebrating Peru's independence from Spanish colonial rule with patriotic events and festivities nationwide.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi
festivals.description: Religious festival featuring processions of saints and sacred relics through the streets of Cusco with indigenous spiritual elements.

festivals.name: Inti Raymi
festivals.description: Spectacular recreation of the Inca winter solstice festival with thousands of performers in traditional costumes at Sacsayhuamán fortress.

festivals.name: Qoyllur Rit'i
festivals.description: Ancient indigenous pilgrimage to a glacier in the Andes where thousands gather for spiritual ceremonies and to witness the 'star snow'.

festivals.name: San Juan Festival
festivals.description: Amazonian celebration of Saint John with bonfires, traditional music, and indigenous ceremonies honoring water and fertility.

festivals.name: Festival of the Virgin of Carmen
festivals.description: Religious festival honoring the patron saint with processions, traditional dances like the Paucartambo dance, and indigenous ceremonies.

festivals.name: Festival of San Santiago
festivals.description: Celebration honoring Saint James with traditional dances, bullfights, and regional festivities along the northern coast.

festivals.name: Assumption of Mary
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrating the Assumption of the Virgin Mary with religious observances and community gatherings.

festivals.name: Fiesta Patria
festivals.description: National battle commemoration celebrating Peru's military victory with parades and patriotic ceremonies.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: Religious observance where Peruvians honor deceased loved ones with visits to gravesites and traditional foods.

festivals.name: Día de Muertos Market
festivals.description: Highland celebration where families prepare special foods and offerings for deceased relatives with indigenous spiritual traditions.

festivals.name: Immaculate Conception
festivals.description: National public holiday and religious festival celebrating the Immaculate Conception of Mary with processions and observances.

festivals.name: Santuranticuy
festivals.description: Ancient Quechua Christmas tradition in Cusco's plaza where artisans sell religious figurines and handicrafts for nativity scenes.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrating the birth of Jesus Christ with religious services and family gatherings throughout Peru.

festivals.name: Festival of San Pedro y San Pablo
festivals.description: Coastal celebration honoring the patron saints with colorful processions, fishing traditions, and regional music and dance.

festivals.name: Arequipa Foundation Day
festivals.description: Regional celebration of Arequipa's founding with parades, cultural events, and festivities showcasing the city's heritage.

festivals.name: Huamanga Festival
festivals.description: Festival celebrating Peruvian artisan crafts with displays of textiles, ceramics, and traditional crafts from the central highlands.

festivals.name: Holy Week in Ayacucho
festivals.description: One of Peru's most important religious celebrations featuring elaborate processions, palm ceremonies, and traditional highland religious observances.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Francisco de Asís
festivals.description: Religious celebration honoring Saint Francis of Assisi with masses and religious processions in Lima and other regions.

festivals.name: Peruano Festival of Colors
festivals.description: Vibrant cultural festival in Lima celebrating Peruvian art, music, dance, and crafts with performances and exhibitions.

festivals.name: Mistura Food Festival
festivals.description: Premier gastronomic festival showcasing Peru's diverse cuisine with famous chefs, cooking demonstrations, and tasting experiences.

festivals.name: Virgen del Carmen Celebration
festivals.description: Religious festival featuring traditional boat processions on Lake Titicaca and indigenous dances honoring the Virgin of Carmen.

festivals.name: Andean Weaving Festival
festivals.description: Festival celebrating traditional Andean textile techniques with workshops, exhibitions, and demonstrations by indigenous weavers.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Chocolate
festivals.description: Festival celebrating Peruvian cacao and chocolate with tastings, chocolate-making demonstrations, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Lima Fashion Week
festivals.description: International fashion event showcasing contemporary Peruvian designers and traditional textile artisans on the runway.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Martín
festivals.description: Religious festival honoring Saint Martin with traditional dances and processions in the Puno region.

festivals.name: Wititi Dance Festival
festivals.description: Traditional dance festival featuring the historic Wititi courtship dance performed by communities around Lake Titicaca.

festivals.name: National Contest of Peruvian Paso Horse
festivals.description: Elite competition showcasing Peru's iconic Paso horse breed with demonstrations of traditional horsemanship and breeding.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Vendimia
festivals.description: Wine harvest festival in the Arequipa wine region celebrating local viticulture with tastings and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Marinera Championship
festivals.description: National competition celebrating Peru's national dance the Marinera with dancers from across the country competing at the highest level.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Isidro
festivals.description: Religious celebration honoring the patron saint of farmers with processions, agricultural displays, and community festivities.

festivals.name: Encuentro Internacional de Escritores
festivals.description: International writers' festival bringing together authors from around the world for readings, workshops, and literary discussions.

festivals.name: Fiesta de San Antonio de Padua
festivals.description: Highland celebration honoring Saint Anthony of Padua with traditional music, dance, and religious processions in the mountain communities.
